summary: Iteration 114 frontend E2E for Chat module. Core Chat flow PASSES end-to-end for Abhijit (implant_incharge): hamburger-btn → drawer-forum → /forum → segment-chat → /forum/chat → All Staff group → PHI banner shown → chat-input + chat-send-btn work → message appears in thread. Create-group flow also WORKS: /forum/chat → new-group-btn → /forum/chat/create → group-name-input + add-members-btn + create-group-submit testids all resolve; group 'E2E Iter114 Group' created and redirected to /forum/chat/687821cb-... ; message 'iter114-created-group-msg' posted successfully. HOWEVER two material issues surfaced: (1) CRITICAL REGRESSION in the Discussion Forum thread screen — opening any thread (e.g. 'Test CBCT Patient') crashes with 'ReferenceError: Cannot access `load` before initialization' originating from /app/frontend/app/forum/[threadId].tsx (useFocusEffect at L118-121 references `load` in its deps array before `load` is declared). This regressed the discussion-forum regression requirement. (2) Nurse-drawer-hidden assertion could not be definitively validated: nurse.1 logs in and lands on /whatsnew where no hamburger-btn exists; a backend /api/dashboard 403 blocks automatic advance to /dashboard. When the test forced page.goto('/dashboard'), drawer-forum testid WAS observed for the nurse (contradicts the requirement) — but this may be a side-effect of the stale auth state after the 403 / forced navigation. Main agent should fix the threadId.tsx crash first and verify nurse-gating on a clean onboarding flow. All other chat testIDs are now correctly exposed (iteration-113's RNW propagation fixes hold).

ui_bugs.issue: CRITICAL: Uncaught ReferenceError 'Cannot access load before initialization' at L75:44 / L121:7. useFocusEffect(useCallback(() => { const t = setInterval(() => { load(); }, 15000); return () => clearInterval(t); }, [load])) at lines 118-121 references `load` in its dependency array before `load` is declared (load is declared later as a useCallback further down the file). On web this causes the entire ForumThreadScreen to crash inside the error boundary. Net effect: clicking any discussion-forum thread from /forum renders the Metro dev error overlay and navigates back to /forum. This regresses the Discussion Forum read/reply flow.

When nurse.1@dental.edu logs in, they land on /whatsnew (no hamburger) and /api/dashboard returns 403 for nurses. When the test forced navigation to /dashboard, hamburger-btn appeared and [data-testid='drawer-forum'] was present in the drawer for the nurse — contradicting the 'Nurses excluded from Discussion Forum' requirement. This may be caused by: (a) AuthContext user object not being hydrated because /api/dashboard (or an auth-bootstrap call) returns 403, leaving role as undefined so the isNurse filter does not hide the item; OR (b) nurse-gating only works on the natural post-login flow which we could not drive because /whatsnew has no Skip/Continue testid. Main agent should (i) give /whatsnew a continue/skip testid, (ii) ensure user.role is available before DrawerMenu renders (e.g. null-check or loading gate), (iii) confirm on real device that the Discussion Forum entry is absent for nurse.

Member rows in the Add Members modal do NOT expose per-row testIDs (no member-option-*, user-option-*, or member-row-* attributes discoverable from DOM scan of 109 testids). The modal also has no member-modal-done / modal-close testid — only a text 'Done' button in the header. Test could not programmatically add a member via testid; we still created the group (owner-only, '1 members') by submitting without selecting. Please add `data-testid="member-option-{userId}"` to each row and `data-testid="member-modal-done"` to the Done button to make this deterministically testable.
ui_bugs.selector: Add Members modal rows and Done button

ui_bugs.issue: Minor: repeated console error 'Unexpected text node: . A text node cannot be a child of a <View>.' fires twice every time the drawer opens — indicates a stray whitespace inside a <View> in DrawerMenu. Not blocking but polluting logs.

integration_issues.issue: Clicking the 'Test CBCT Patient' thread from /forum list triggers the ForumThreadScreen crash above; no Case Summary renders and the reply composer is unreachable.

/api/dashboard returns 403 for nurse role, which triggers 'Failed to load dashboard' uncaught error and leaves nurse stuck on /whatsnew with no visible hamburger. Either allow a nurse-scoped dashboard or redirect nurses to a nurse-home without calling /api/dashboard.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix /app/frontend/app/forum/[threadId].tsx — move the `useFocusEffect(useCallback(() => { ... load(); ... }, [load]))` block to AFTER the `const load = useCallback(...)` declaration so `load` is initialized before being referenced in the deps array. Without this fix, the Discussion Forum regression is broken.

Fix nurse-role drawer gating: ensure `user.role` is hydrated before DrawerMenu renders (loading gate or conditional render). Verify by actually logging in as nurse.1 and confirming [data-testid='drawer-forum'] is NOT in the DOM.
Give /whatsnew a Skip/Continue button with data-testid='whatsnew-continue' so automated tests can drive nurses past the onboarding screen.
Add per-row data-testid='member-option-{userId}' in forum/chat/create.tsx Add Members modal and data-testid='member-modal-done' on the Done button.
Investigate /api/dashboard 403 for nurse role — decide whether to allow nurses or reroute them.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/forum/[threadId].tsx L118-121: TDZ violation — `load` used in useCallback deps before declaration; production crash on first mount. This should have been caught by the `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` ESLint rule or a simple smoke test on /forum/{id}.

/app/frontend/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx L58-60: isNurse filter relies on user.role being populated synchronously; without a guard on user === null, nurses whose auth bootstrap fails (e.g. 403 on /api/dashboard) will see the full admin drawer. Add `if (!user) return null;` or skeleton state.

/app/frontend/app/forum/chat/create.tsx: Add Members modal lacks row-level testIDs — makes automated member-picking impossible and inconsistent with the rest of the chat module which has comprehensive testid coverage.

success_rate.frontend: Test1 (All Staff E2E) PASS ✓, Test2 (Create Group + send message) PASS ✓, Test3 (Nurse drawer hidden) INCONCLUSIVE/LIKELY-FAIL — drawer-forum observed for nurse after forced /dashboard nav, Test4 (Forum thread regression) FAIL — ForumThreadScreen crashes

seed_data_creation: Created chat group 'E2E Iter114 Group' (id 687821cb-2444-46d6-83c6-fdf5eb1fd6e5) with one owner member and one message 'iter114-created-group-msg'. Also posted 'iter114-AllStaff-msg' into the All Staff group (id 320e2a96-c779-49e6-ba66-2a996d8f604c) and 'E2E-iter114-test-msg' into TEST_ChatGroup_112.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Metro dev-error overlay (#error-overlay) can intercept clicks once any crash fires in the session — dismiss with `await page.evaluate("() => document.getElementById('error-overlay')?.remove()")` or use force=True. Abhijit lands on /dashboard; nurse.1 lands on /whatsnew (no hamburger, no Skip testid). To reach the chat module: hamburger-btn → drawer-forum → segment-chat → chat-group-* / new-group-btn. Chat testids (chat-input, chat-send-btn, chat-group-{id}, new-group-btn, group-name-input, add-members-btn, create-group-submit, hamburger-btn, drawer-forum, segment-chat, segment-forum) all resolve. Add Members modal rows lack testids — use text-based selection or add testids. The 'Test CBCT Patient' thread currently CRASHES — don't use it for regression until the load-before-init bug is fixed.
rca_of_the_issue: 1) ForumThreadScreen crash — TDZ (temporal dead zone) on `const load = useCallback(...)`. Line 118-121 uses `load` in a useFocusEffect deps array before the `const load` declaration further down the function body. JS hoists `const` but the binding is in TDZ until initialized, so on first render React executes the useFocusEffect hook (which evaluates deps), touches `load`, and throws ReferenceError. Fix: reorder so `const load = useCallback(...)` is declared before any hook that references it. 2) Nurse-drawer-visible — user.role undefined at first DrawerMenu render because /api/dashboard 403 leaves auth bootstrap in an error state; isNurse evaluates to false so the full drawer renders. Fix: gate DrawerMenu on user !== null || role defined.
